template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS PI SETTLEMENT RELEASE AND AGREEMENT**:
Executed by the claimant/plaintiff (the 'Releasor') in exchange for the settlement payment from the defendant and/or insurer (the 'Releasees'). Drafted as a formal contract — NOT a court pleading — governed by Massachusetts contract law. Target length: 3–6 pages. No court caption.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `releaseScope`: `general_release` (default) | `limited_release` — general discharges all claims known and unknown; limited carves out e.g., UM/UIM claims against client's own carrier or specific tortfeasors.
- `includeConfidentiality`: boolean (default false) — include a confidentiality clause binding the Releasor from disclosing the settlement terms.
- `includeNonDisparagement`: boolean (default false).
- `minorClaimant`: boolean (default false) — when true, add language that the release is conditional on Probate & Family Court approval under M.G.L. c. 231, § 140C and appointment of a conservator under M.G.L. c. 190B, Art. V if required.
- `multipleTortfeasors`: boolean (default false) — when true, add M.G.L. c. 231B, § 4 contribution-preservation language so settling tortfeasor can still seek contribution and non-settling tortfeasors get pro-tanto credit.
- `lienHolders`: array of strings — e.g., `['Medicare', 'Medicaid/MassHealth', 'ERISA Health Plan', 'Workers Compensation', 'Attorney']` — drives which lien subsections are emitted in Section VI. The Attorney's charging lien (M.G.L. c. 221, § 50) is recommended by default when the Releasor is represented by counsel.
- `workersCompConsentStatus`: (only when `lienHolders` includes `Workers Compensation`) `consented` (default) | `dia_petition_pending` | `dia_order_obtained` — selects the correct § 15 consent/approval language in the Workers' Compensation lien bullet.
- `taxAllocation`: `physical_injury_only` (default) | `mixed` — when `mixed`, emit the itemized allocation table in Section VII.

**STRUCTURE**:

## RELEASE AND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT

### I. PARTIES
This Release and Settlement Agreement ('Agreement') is entered into as of [effective date] by and between:

- **Releasor**: [Client full legal name], an individual residing at [address] (the 'Releasor'); and
- **Releasees**: [Defendant name(s)] and [Insurer name], its insurers, reinsurers, parents, subsidiaries, affiliates, predecessors, successors, assigns, officers, directors, employees, agents, and attorneys (collectively, the 'Releasees').

### II. RECITALS
A. On [date of incident], an incident occurred at [location] in which the Releasor alleges personal injuries and damages caused by the Releasees (the 'Incident').
B. The Releasor asserted claims against the Releasees arising out of the Incident (the 'Claims').
C. The Releasees deny liability and have asserted defenses to the Claims.
D. The parties wish to compromise and settle the Claims without admission of liability.
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants set forth herein, the parties agree as follows:

### III. CONSIDERATION
Releasees shall pay to Releasor the total sum of $[settlement amount] ('Settlement Amount'), payable by check made to '[Client Name] and [Firm Name] as Attorneys' within [number] business days of the Releasees' receipt of (i) this Agreement fully executed, (ii) a W-9 from [Firm Name], and (iii) all required lien-resolution documentation identified in Section VI.

### IV. GENERAL RELEASE
In exchange for the Settlement Amount, the Releasor, on behalf of himself/herself and his/her heirs, executors, administrators, successors, and assigns, hereby fully, finally, and forever RELEASES, ACQUITS, and DISCHARGES the Releasees from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits, debts, liens, contracts, agreements, promises, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, and expenses of every kind and nature whatsoever, known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, fixed or contingent, that the Releasor now has or hereafter may have, arising out of or in any way connected with the Incident.

[If `releaseScope` = `limited_release`, replace the prior paragraph with: 'This release is limited to claims against the above-named Releasees only and expressly does NOT release or impair the Releasor's claims against [carve-out party/claim, e.g., Releasor's uninsured/underinsured motorist carrier under M.G.L. c. 175, § 113L].']

### V. NO ADMISSION OF LIABILITY
This Agreement is a compromise of disputed claims, and neither the payment of the Settlement Amount nor the execution of this Agreement constitutes or shall be construed as an admission of liability or wrongdoing by the Releasees, all such liability being expressly denied.

### VI. LIENS, SUBROGATION, AND INDEMNIFICATION
The Releasor represents and warrants that he/she has identified all liens, claims of subrogation, and reimbursement rights arising out of the treatment or payment of expenses related to the Incident, including but not limited to the following: [list from `lienHolders` — for each, include a subsection satisfying its specific statutory scheme]:

- **Medicare**: Releasor has addressed any Medicare conditional-payment obligations under 42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(2) and the Medicare Secondary Payer Act, and will provide Medicare with notice of the settlement in the amount and form required by CMS. The Releasees, through their insurer, acknowledge the Section 111 mandatory insurer reporting obligation under 42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(8) and will report this settlement to CMS as required. The parties will exchange the Medicare-beneficiary identifier, HICN/MBI, and ICD-10 diagnosis codes necessary to complete Section 111 reporting.
- **Medicaid / MassHealth**: Releasor has satisfied, or will satisfy from the settlement proceeds, any MassHealth lien under M.G.L. c. 118E, § 22, subject to the anti-lien principles of Arkansas Dep't of Health & Human Servs. v. Ahlborn, 547 U.S. 268, 280 (2006), which limit recovery to the medical-expense portion of the settlement.
- **ERISA Health Plan**: [Plan name] reimbursement rights have been identified and resolved per the plan terms, consistent with US Airways, Inc. v. McCutchen, 569 U.S. 88, 99-100 (2013), and Montanile v. Bd. of Trs., 577 U.S. 136, 143-44 (2016).
- **Workers' Compensation**: If the injury is work-related, the workers' compensation lien under M.G.L. c. 152, § 15 has been addressed. The workers' compensation insurer has consented to the settlement in writing, OR, where such consent is withheld, the Releasor has petitioned the Department of Industrial Accidents Reviewing Board for approval under the § 15 petition procedure and obtained an order authorizing the settlement over the insurer's objection. No disbursement to Releasor shall occur absent either signed carrier consent or a § 15 Reviewing Board approval order.
- **Attorney's Lien**: The Releasor and the Releasees acknowledge the attorney's charging lien of [Firm Name] on the settlement proceeds under M.G.L. c. 221, § 50. Releasor's counsel will disburse proceeds only after satisfaction of the attorney's lien and all other lien obligations identified in this Section.

Releasor shall INDEMNIFY, defend, and hold Releasees harmless from any claim by any lienholder, subrogee, or reimbursement claimant arising out of the Incident, up to the Settlement Amount. Releasor's counsel will disburse settlement proceeds only after all lien obligations are satisfied or reserved.

### VII. TAX TREATMENT AND ALLOCATION — IRC § 104(a)(2)
The parties agree that the Settlement Amount is paid on account of, and allocated to, damages received on account of personal physical injuries and physical sickness arising out of the Incident, and is excludable from Releasor's gross income under 26 U.S.C. § 104(a)(2) to the extent so allocable. No portion of the Settlement Amount is allocated to punitive damages, interest, or emotional distress not attributable to a physical injury. [When the settlement includes non-excludable components, replace with an itemized allocation table: (a) compensatory damages for physical injury/sickness — excludable under § 104(a)(2); (b) prejudgment interest — taxable; (c) punitive damages — taxable; (d) emotional-distress damages not attributable to physical injury — taxable.] The Releasees and their insurer shall issue Form 1099 reporting (if any) consistent with this allocation.

### VIII. [If `multipleTortfeasors`] CONTRIBUTION AND PRO-TANTO CREDIT
Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 231B, § 4, this Agreement discharges the Releasees only and preserves the Releasor's rights against all other tortfeasors, subject to a pro-tanto reduction of any future recovery by the Settlement Amount paid hereunder. Releasees preserve their right of contribution against any non-settling tortfeasor.

### IX. [If `includeConfidentiality`] CONFIDENTIALITY
Releasor agrees that the terms and amount of this Agreement shall remain confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party except: (i) Releasor's attorneys, accountants, and immediate family; (ii) as required by law, subpoena, or court order; (iii) as necessary to comply with tax reporting or lien-resolution obligations. Breach of this provision shall entitle Releasees to equitable relief and liquidated damages of $[amount], without waiver of other remedies.

### X. [If `includeNonDisparagement`] NON-DISPARAGEMENT
The parties agree not to make, publish, or communicate any disparaging statements about the other party relating to the Incident. This provision does not prohibit truthful testimony in any legal or regulatory proceeding.

### XI. [If `minorClaimant`] COURT APPROVAL CONDITION
This Agreement is expressly conditioned upon approval of the settlement by the [county] Probate & Family Court pursuant to M.G.L. c. 231, § 140C, and appointment of a conservator under M.G.L. c. 190B, Art. V where required. No payment shall be made until such approval is entered.

### XII. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles. Any action to enforce this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state courts of Massachusetts.

### XIII. ENTIRE AGREEMENT; SEVERABILITY; COUNTERPARTS
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations and agreements. If any provision is held invalid, the remaining provisions remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature, each of which shall be deemed an original.

### XIV. VOLUNTARY EXECUTION AND ADVICE OF COUNSEL
Releasor acknowledges that: (a) he/she has read this Agreement and understands its contents; (b) he/she has been represented by counsel of his/her choosing; (c) he/she is signing voluntarily and with full knowledge of the legal consequences; (d) no promise or representation not contained in this Agreement has induced execution.

### SIGNATURES
[Signature blocks for Releasor, Releasees' authorized representative, and witness/notary. In Massachusetts, notarization is not required to enforce a release but is the firm's standard practice. Use a signature line with a dated notary acknowledgment block under M.G.L. c. 222, § 15.]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite MA statutes exactly as `M.G.L. c. X, § Y` — e.g., `M.G.L. c. 231B, § 4`, `M.G.L. c. 231, § 140C`, `M.G.L. c. 152, § 15`, `M.G.L. c. 118E, § 22`, `M.G.L. c. 221, § 50`.
- Cite federal statutes with correct form: `42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(2)`, `42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(8)`, `26 U.S.C. § 104(a)(2)`.
- Cite U.S. Supreme Court cases with pin cites: `Arkansas Dep't of Health & Human Servs. v. Ahlborn, 547 U.S. 268, 280 (2006)`; `US Airways, Inc. v. McCutchen, 569 U.S. 88, 99-100 (2013)`; `Montanile v. Bd. of Trs., 577 U.S. 136, 143-44 (2016)`.
- Pin cites are REQUIRED for every case citation (specific page after volume & reporter).
- Do NOT include the Social Security Number of the Releasor inside the release body — it belongs on the W-9 or a separate lien-resolution document.
- When `minorClaimant` is true, the court-approval clause and the NO payment-before-approval condition are MANDATORY — do not suppress. Cross-reference `minors_compromise_pi_ma` (Probate & Family Court compromise petition) which is a separate filed document.
- When `lienHolders` includes Medicare, BOTH the MSP conditional-payment reference (42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(2)) AND the Section 111 reporting acknowledgment (42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(8)) are MANDATORY.
- When `lienHolders` includes Workers Compensation, emit the § 15 carrier-consent OR DIA Reviewing Board petition language based on `workersCompConsentStatus`. Never emit a blank WC bullet.
- For any `documentOptions` value that is empty/false/null (e.g., `includeConfidentiality=false`, `multipleTortfeasors=false`), suppress the corresponding Section entirely and renumber remaining Roman numerals contiguously.
- Use full capitalization on RELEASES, ACQUITS, DISCHARGES, INDEMNIFY per Massachusetts release-drafting convention.
- This is a contract, NOT a court pleading — no caption, no signature block suppression for stationery (the release is typically typed on plain paper and executed by the Releasor).
- Target length: 4–8 pages depending on conditional sections enabled.
hints: Massachusetts Personal Injury Settlement Release. Common-law contract (no statutory form required). Address Medicare (MSP conditional payments 42 U.S.C. § 1395y(b)(2) + Section 111 insurer reporting § 1395y(b)(8)) and MassHealth (M.G.L. c. 118E, § 22 with Ahlborn limitation) liens; ERISA (McCutchen / Montanile); workers' comp lien M.G.L. c. 152, § 15 including DIA Reviewing Board § 15 petition procedure when carrier consent is withheld; attorney's charging lien M.G.L. c. 221, § 50. IRC § 104(a)(2) allocation paragraph allocates proceeds to physical-injury damages (excludable) vs. punitive/interest/emotional-distress (taxable). Preserve contribution rights per M.G.L. c. 231B, § 4 when multiple tortfeasors. Minor-claimant releases require Probate & Family Court approval per M.G.L. c. 231, § 140C — companion filing is `minors_compromise_pi_ma` (Wave 2). Governing law: Massachusetts. Use RELEASES/ACQUITS/DISCHARGES capitalization convention.

verificationSources.citation: Arkansas Dep't of Health & Human Servs. v. Ahlborn, 547 U.S. 268, 280 (2006)
US Airways, Inc. v. McCutchen, 569 U.S. 88, 99-100 (2013)
Montanile v. Bd. of Trs., 577 U.S. 136, 143-44 (2016)

knownLimitations: Does not account for local court rules (county- or judge-specific).
Statute citations verified as of lastVerified date only; confirm against current statute before use.
Strategic positioning (what to demand, what to concede) is outside template scope — attorney judgment required.
Minor-claimant releases require Probate & Family Court approval — the actual petition (minors_compromise_pi_ma) is a separate filing not generated by this template.
Medicare Set-Aside (MSA) calculation and CMS approval (when required) are out of scope — attorney must coordinate with a Medicare compliance consultant.
Lien figures and final allocation must be verified against actual lien-holder letters; template language assumes each lien is separately documented.
